steps.title: Identify the signal that sets up examples
steps.content: The phrase "in several ways" establishes that the next sentence will provide specific instances or examples of how the station serves the community.

steps.title: Select the transition that introduces examples
steps.content:
The logical relationship is example: "in several ways" sets up a general claim, and the second sentence delivers specific instances (local news, music, and Umatilla-language programming). The blank needs a transition word signaling that the following sentence is a concrete example of those ways.
  • Choice A — "For instance,": signals that a specific example of the preceding general claim follows, so it fits. ✓
  • Choice B — "As a result,": signals a cause-and-effect consequence, so it fails because the programming is not caused by serving in several ways — it is the service itself. ✘
  • Choice C — "In the end,": signals a final outcome or conclusion, so it fails because no process is being concluded here. ✘
  • Choice D — "By contrast,": signals opposition, so it fails because the second sentence aligns with, not opposes, the first. ✘
That matches choice A — A is correct.
